channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on Sarah McBride, the first openly trans person elected to Congress
item.description: Sarah McBride made history in Delaware, as the first openly transgender state senator in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Stanley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A guide to funeral services for former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Funeral services honoring former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at age 100, will be held in Georgia and Washington, D.C., beginning Saturday, Jan. 4, and concluding Thursday, Jan. 9. Here's a list of events, as provided by the Joint Task Force-National Capital Region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas public safety department announces systemwide outage, closes driver license offices
item.description: The outage appeared only to affect the public safety department. Websites for other state agencies were operational Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Stephen Simpson, KPRC 2

item.title: DNC chair candidate calls for greater investment in GOP states
item.description: In the four-point platform that was first seen by The Hill, Skoufis vowed to “dramatically” enhance the party’s Red State Fund if elected chair.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: The Indiana National Guard says a 34-year-old soldier serving in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden closes government as 'mark of respect' for late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Monday signed an executive order closing executive offices of the government for late former President Jimmy Carter's funeral on Jan. 9.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces nearly $2.5 billion more in military aid for Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that the United States will send nearly $2.5 billion more in weapons to Ukraine as his administration works quickly to spend all the money it has available to help Kyiv fight off Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's Defense Department announces new multibillion-dollar aid packages for Ukraine
item.description: The Biden White House announced billions of dollars of additional military assistance to Ukraine in a final push ahead of the impending Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An appeals court upholds a $5 million award in a sexual abuse verdict against President-elect Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal appeals court on Monday upheld a jury’s finding in a civil case that Donald Trump sexually abused a columnist in an upscale department store dressing room in the mid-1990s. The 2nd U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter ‘killer rabbit attack’ story highlighted his struggles as president
item.description: After the passing of 100-year-old former President Jimmy Carter, many are recalling the “killer rabbit" incident in which Carter had to fight off a berserk swamp creature while fishing in his hometown of Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is an example of ‘simple decency': Joe Biden
item.description: President Biden said former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday, “lived a life measured not by words but by deeds.
item.dc:creator: Brian Howard, NewsNation

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Photos: Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center. Here's a look at his life and times in photos. He had turned 100 in October, making him the longest-lived president in the nation’s history.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Former President Carter, the unassuming Georgia peanut farmer whose improbable political rise took him to the governor’s mansion before the White House, died peacefully on Sunday in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Scott Wong, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC
